description: A-Alpha leverages its platform technology AlphaSeq to generate high-quality protein interaction data at scale, enabling the discovery of better drugs faster.

Adimab has initiated over 350 antibody discovery campaigns with more than 80 partners, ranging from leading academic institutions to top-20 pharma companies, delivering a diverse panel of tailored antibodies to these partners' target product profiles.
Adyn provides medically actionable scientific insights and empowers individuals to proactively optimize their health—from birth control, to fertility, and beyond.

Aera was started by a distinguished set of industry leaders and company builders to expand the reach and impact of genetic medicines. Aera’s mission is to harness enabling delivery technologies and precision payloads to develop transformative genetic medicines.‍

Aeva is building the next generation sensing system for autonomous vehicles that is safe, simple, and scalable. Aeva’s product combines the key advantages of LiDAR, Radar, Motion Sensing, and Vision in a single compact system. Aeva’s unique sensing technology uses continuous low-power light to sense the instant velocity of every point per frame at long ranges. This produces a 4D map of the environment where the instant velocity information drastically enhances the detection and classification of all critical objects such as pedestrians, bicycles, and vehicles at distance.
Ahana's managed cloud service allows organizations of all shapes and sizes to easily use PrestoDB, a popular open source federated SQL engine.

AirMap organizes and provides accurate, dynamic, and trustworthy airspace information for drone makers, operators and regulators so people know when, where, and how to fly drones legally and safely. Integrating multiple sources of reliable data, AirMap gives pilots an easy-to-use, yet detailed solution, providing a single view of restricted area. The company’s fully-digital map shows only the airspace rules that impact UAS operators, stripping away the clutter of higher altitude restrictions and details. AirMap provides airspace information via an API and a downloadable app, and manages the website NoFlyZone.org, where people can register their homes if they do not want drones to fly on their properties.
Alife Health is building artificial intelligence tools to help in vitro fertilization (IVF) physicians maximize a patient’s chances of success while lowering costs and barriers to access.

AllStripes aggregates and analyzes medical records and sells the de-identified data to pharmaceutical companies to help them develop medicines. In exchange for access to the data, patients get their fragmented medical records organized into an app they can use to track their treatment and get second opinions.

Alluvium empowers organizations by deploying machine intelligence as close to the data generation as possible, and tying in human expertise to continuously build operational and institutional knowledge.

Backed by Caffeinated Capital, Founders Fund, Lux Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, and others, Amca has acquired its first supplier—Electro-Mech Components, Inc. in South El Monte, CA—and counts Boeing among its customers for its line of human-machine interface products.
Anagenex's technology analyzes 100-1000x more compounds compared to traditional approaches by combining machine learning with DNA Encoded Libraries.
Anchorage provides a new standard for digital asset custody by applying the latest advances in modern security engineering to deliver the services institutions expect from a traditional custodian.

Anduril brings Silicon Valley talent and funding to the defense sector, combining cutting-edge AI software with the latest hardware to solve complex national security challenges for America and its allies.

Applied Compute builds Specific Intelligence for enterprises, unlocking the knowledge inside a company to train custom models and deploy an in-house agent workforce.‍Today’s state-of-the-art AI isn’t one-size-fits-all—it’s a tailored system that continuously learns from a company’s processes, data, expertise, and goals. The same way companies compete today by having the best human workforce, the companies building for the future will compete by having the best agent workforce supporting their human bosses.‍Applied Compute a small, talent-dense team of engineers, researchers, and operators who have built some of the most influential AI systems in the world, including reinforcement learning infrastructure at OpenAI and data foundations at Scale AI, with additional experience from Together, Two Sigma, and Watershed.

Applied Intuition, Inc. is powering the future of physical AI. Founded in 2017 and now valued at $15 billion, the Silicon Valley company is creating the digital infrastructure needed to bring intelligence to every moving machine on the planet. Applied Intuition services the automotive, defense, trucking, construction, mining and agriculture industries in three core areas: tools and infrastructure, operating systems, and autonomy. Eighteen of the top 20 global automakers, as well as the United States military and its allies, trust the company's solutions to deliver physical intelligence.‍

Aptible is a cloud-based platform designed to automate HIPAA compliance for medical and healthcare companies. Aptible manages the chore of documenting infrastructure so mobile health companies can focus on solving the healthcare needs of their customers.

Armada is on a mission to redefine boundaries with the world's first full stack edge computing platform. With infrastructure like no other, conceived and built by the proven leaders in our industry, Armada enables industries to rapidly deploy, operate and monitor a complete data center in the remote corners of the world.
Astranis is developing novel satellite technology that will provide a connectivity solution to those in underdeveloped and sparsely populated areas.

Atavistik Bio’s high-throughput platform for the unbiased identification of metabolite-protein interactions will enable us to discover first-in-class drugs inspired and empowered by nature’s own mechanisms of allosteric control.

Atrandi Biosciences is advancing the frontier of single-cell analysis with its proprietary Semi-Permeable Capsule (SPC) technology. The platform encapsulates hundreds of thousands of cells in permeable microenvironments, enabling complex, multi-step reactions at scale while preserving single-cell resolution. By combining throughput with precision, Atrandi unlocks true multiomic insights and accelerates biological discovery. Founded in 2016, Atrandi - meaning “you discover” in Lithuanian -embodies the company’s mission to empower scientists to reveal the complexity of life.‍
Auransa is redefining precision medicine by using AI and genomics to develop treatments that address the unmet needs of significant patient subpopulations.

Auris Health's vision begins with its Monarch Platform, an FDA-approved device that uses the latest advancements in robotics, software, data science and endoscope innovation to enter the body non-invasively to root out lung cancer.

In 2012, while still in graduate school, Aurora Solar co-founders Sam Adeyemo and Chris Hopper installed a commercial solar system for a school in East Africa. The project was a resounding success, allowing the school to save on its electricity bills as well as have a reliable and carbon-free source of energy. Talking to other solar installers, they found that soft-costs were impeding the growth of the industry. Based on these experiences, they founded Aurora with the goal of enabling any solar professional to design and sell an optimal solar project in minutes. Now, it's been used for more than 5 million projects and growing rapidly.

Avail minimizes the number of people who have to be physically present in hospitals’ operating rooms through HIPAA-compliant technology that enables necessary experts to collaborate virtually in real time.
Babylon Bio is on a mission to cure Alzheimer's. Babylon finances development of its moonshot programs for Alzheimer's through a flip-and-fuel approach.

Basic Capital is an end-to-end retirement benefits platform; handling all aspects of 401(k) setup, administration, and employee experience. In addition to thousands of traditional investment options, Basic Capital gives your team access to more investing power.
Benchling provides a unified R&D platform that accelerates, measures, and forecasts R&D – from discovery through bioprocessing – all in one place.

Bluenote is transforming the status quo by enabling life sciences organizations to accelerate the most laborious aspects of their operations. Bluenote’s AI platform for life sciences is driving the industry’s shift to AI-first operations. From content automation for pre-clinical study reports, INDs, NDAs, BLAs, CSRs, CMC and PMAs, to AI agents for lab operations and quality, to analysis over protocols and vast experimental data, Bluenote is accelerating mission-critical workflows by 50-75%.

Braid Health has built the first frictionless infrastructure for healthcare data and collaboration: FDA-cleared, secure, real-time, modality-agnostic, and AI-augmented. With products in areas of 1) interoperability and care coordination for IDNs, providers, and patients; 2) collaboration for multidisciplinary conferences and tumor boards; 3) AI-supported radiology software and service; and 4) a reimagined data flywheel for clinical trials and pharma R&D, Braid Health is unlocking the flow of healthcare data for the betterment of patient care.

The company's first product, Brainiac Kids, is formulated to meet the growing consumer demand for healthier, delicious yogurt options with important added benefits that support brain development of children.
Bright Machines is bringing together intelligent software with flexible factory robots and machine learning to help customers meet the growing demands of the next generation of manufacturing.
Buf's mission is to deprecate REST/JSON and move the industry towards a future of schema driven development with Protobuf.
Cajal Therapeutics is dedicated to developing innovative medicines for serious diseases, including anemias of inflammation, neurodegeneration, and other iron-related disorders.‍
Cala Health is a medical technology company developing a new class of electrical medicine, called neuroperipheral therapy, in order to treat chronic disease with body-worn electronics.
Cape Analytics Inc. was founded in 2014 by Ryan Kottenstette to provide geospatial structured data and aerial imagery for insurance and property underwriting.
Caper builds smart shopping systems - powered by deep learning and computer vision - that dramatically improve the retail experience. The company's first products target the grocery sector.

Leveraging its unique technology platform, Carbon Health provides its patients with omnichannel care, designed to meet patients where they are by delivering care via a variety of access points, including in-person clinics, home-based care, hardware, on-site employer-based care, and virtually via the Carbon Health app. Carbon Health also provides value-based care to enterprise partners across a broad range of industries, including academia, sports, entertainment, biotechnology, and travel.

Using the science of nanotechnology, Cerulean is developing a suite of potent new cancer drugs that selectively target tumors, burrowing their way into cancer cells while leaving healthy cells unaffected. Clinical results indicate the drugs have the potential to be effective across numerous cancer types, while improving both safety and quality-of-life for patients. The company’s lead drug is currently being evaluated in Phase II clinical trials. The drug platform has also shown promise in delivering gene therapy to cancer cells. The company completed its IPO in 2014.

Chronosphere is the observability platform built for control in the modern, containerized world. Chronosphere empowers customers to focus on the data and insights that matter by reducing data complexity, optimizing costs, and remediating issues faster. Its Observability Platform reduces data volumes and associated costs by 60% on average while saving developers thousands of hours. Chronosphere’s Fluent Bit-based Telemetry Pipeline product optimizes and simplifies observability and security log data. The product transforms logs at the source and routes them to any destination without lock-in. Recognized as a leader by major analyst firms, Chronosphere is trusted by the world’s most innovative brands, including Snap, Robinhood, DoorDash, and Zillow.

Citizen is a public safety mobile application company that provides users with real-time information about incidents of crime or other civil disturbances that are reported in the immediate vicinity. The company seeks to provide residents with complete transparency of their own neighborhoods, improving the safety and security of everyday citizens.

Clarifai is an artificial intelligence company that excels in visual recognition and solves real-world problems for businesses and developers alike. The company was founded in 2013 by Dr. Matthew Zeiler, an expert in machine learning. Clarifai has been a market leader since winning every one of the top five places in image classification at the ImageNet 2013 competition. Clarifai’s powerful image and video recognition technology is built on the most advanced machine learning system. The company shares this technology through an API, empowering developers all over the world to build next-generation intelligent applications.

Founded in 2020 by brothers Arjun and Abhijay Bhatnagar, Cloaked is changing the way consumers share their data and brokering new rules for how their information is used -- without changing the way they operate day to day. Cloaked offers instant identifiers and smart settings to make it easy for individuals to choose what, when, where and with whom they share personal information. Using a browser extension and mobile device, users can create unique emails, phone numbers and passwords instantly.

Cognichip is developing the world’s first Artificial Chip Intelligence (ACI®) to reimagine chip design. Led by experts from Amazon, Google, Apple, Synopsys, Aquantia, and KLA, Cognichip is tackling two major challenges in semiconductor development: high cost and inaccessibility. Cognichip’s ACI® introduces a breakthrough, physics-informed AI foundation model, purposely built for semiconductor design. ACI® fundamentally rethinks 40 years of traditional, serial chip development with a modern, AI-first, conversational design approach, leveraging large-scale, secure, accelerated compute infrastructures. From individuals to enterprises, ACI® allows engineering teams to work more efficiently and cost-effectively, elevating artificial intelligence as the new scaling factor for the semiconductor industry.

Cognition is the leading AI coding agent company and makers of Devin, the first AI software engineer. Cognition is building collaborative AI teammates that enable engineers to focus on more interesting problems and empower engineering teams to strive for more ambitious goals. The company is led by a team of world-class engineers, gold medalist coders, former founders, and leaders from companies at the cutting edge of AI.

By rewiring the antiquated processes behind public notice for the benefit of publishers and the clients they serve, Column is helping governments, lawyers, banks, businesses, and even someone looking to change their name file required information in a fast and efficient way. Today, newspaper staff are overwhelmed with email chains of proofs and piles of affidavits, while governments and lawyers find themselves playing phone tag with the newspaper, uncertain how much it will cost or if their public notice will be published in time. Column digitizes and streamlines this entire process, saving costs and time for everyone.
The Commure platform provides a secure, reliable, and interoperable cloud-native foundation atop which developers can build and deploy the next generation of healthcare applications.

ConductorAI’s mission is to fundamentally improve the efficiency of the US Government, particularly in how it interfaces with corporations and partners, by powering AI-augmented approval and review workflows. Its flagship platform, Conduit, supports document-based process improvements at multiple classification levels in both cloud-native and on-premises environments. The company was founded by Zachary Long, Benjamin Fichter, and Eric Schwartz after seeing first-hand at Palantir and PreVeil how challenging it was to share information both within their own organizations and across the government.
Copia builds straightforward tools for automation professionals to reduce downtime, streamline operations, and improve their day-to-day, starting with Git-based revision control for PLC programming.

Coris is fostering greater trust between SMBs and software companies. Unstructured SMB data is growing exponentially, but this information is not being standardized and utilized appropriately. Coris offers AI-driven risk infrastructure software so companies can automate SMB underwriting, risk & fraud management in 46 countries.

Covariant is empowering industrials to perceive and act like humans instead of just strictly following pre-programmed or planned trajectories. Their technology enables existing robot hardware to handle a much wider range of tasks for which existing solutions would otherwise break down, including bin-picking of complex shapes, de-palletizing of irregular stacks and manipulation of deformable objects such as food, wires or cables and fluid-bags.

With powerful, purpose-built data set to drive efficient creation of machine learning models, Creyon engineers oligonucleotide-based medicines (OBMs) for safe, precise gene-centric therapies with unprecedented speed and predictable efficacy and safety.

Crosby is the AI law firm that helps companies execute contracts faster. Crosby combines the speed and intelligence of AI with the safety of lawyers-in-the-loop to review contracts like MSAs, DPAs, and NDAs in minutes. Crosby helps fast-growing businesses like Cursor, Ramp, Cognition, and Clay reach agreement and close deals faster.

Cryptonumerics helps data-driven organizations unlock the data they need to maximize business value and insights in a privacy-protected manner, resulting in full customers’ trust and regulatory compliance.

Crystal IS makes the world's highest performing ultraviolet LEDs for use in water, air, and surface sterilization. The company's breakthrough performance is made possible by low-defect density, single-crystal, aluminum nitride substrates and proprietary technologies for crystal growth.

CTRL-labs has created a transformative brain-machine interface that picks up the neural signals within the body to control devices outside of it, with little more than natural gestures. The next step: non-invasively reading the intention from the brain - no gestures required.
Databricks is the only open and unified data analytics platform that combines multiple disciplines in one place to unify your data teams and help them solve the world’s toughest problems.

Combining smart cameras, motion sensors and an AI processing Hub, Deep Sentinel creates an intelligent surveillance zone around the perimeter of residential properties. The system detects people, animals, cars and other objects and can interpret suspicious behavior in real-time and intervene when necessary to keep the home safe.

Founded by an experienced team that includes MIT professors, serial entrepreneurs, and veterans of CAD software and robotics companies, Desktop Metal seeks to build office-friendly 3D printers that can inexpensively produce parts from a range of high-performance metal alloys. “Metal 3D printing has been out of the reach of most companies because it’s very expensive and slow,” says founder Ric Fulop. “We’re developing a system that’s fast and more accessible.”

What is drone racing? A high speed competitive sport where skilled pilots fly quad-copter drones through three-dimensional courses at speeds up to 120mph. First-person-view goggles display a real-time video feed from the perspective of the drone. Drone Racing League custom-builds drones for speed, agility, and performance and hosts events at iconic locations around the world. Throughout 2016, DRL is hosting a global race series culminating in a World Championship.

DuckDuckGo is an Internet privacy company that empowers you to seamlessly take control of your personal information online, without any tradeoffs. With our roots as the search engine that doesn’t track you, we’ve expanded what we do to protect you no matter where the Internet takes you.

Dyno Therapeutics is pioneering an artificial intelligence (AI) powered approach to gene therapy. Using machine learning and quantitative high-throughput in vivo experimentation, we are inventing new ways to design gene vectors with a focus on cell-targeting capsid proteins from adeno-associated virus (AAV), the most widely-used vector for gene therapies.

Echodyne is developing patented metamaterials technology to build high-performance radars that are smaller, lighter, and cost less than current solutions. Echodyne technology enables small, lightweight radars for UAVs, security, robots and autonomous vehicles that work well even in poor environmental conditions.

eGenesis is advancing genetically engineered porcine organs for human transplant. The company’s lead product (EGEN-2784) is for the treatment of patients with kidney failure. eGenesis has published the most extensive preclinical dataset in the field of cross-species transplantation, demonstrating consistent and durable long-term survival. In March 2024, the company’s lead product was used in the world’s first porcine kidney transplant in a living human patient. eGenesis is also advancing additional programs for patients with heart failure, liver failure.

Eikon’s live cell imaging drug discovery platform is built on groundbreaking innovations from its founders who pioneered the field of in vivo single-molecule microscopy, and whose work was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 2014.
Leveraging breakthrough advancements in machine learning, computational metabolomics, and knowledge graphs, Enveda is discovering the next generation of small molecule therapeutics.

Epsilon3 enables companies in space and complex operations to streamline communication and reduce errors in operations, saving time and money. The company's product allows users to track procedures and critical mission data collaboratively, prevent failures through error checking, and evolve operations over time based on experience.

Erebor is a nationally chartered bank purpose-built for the innovation economy. The company serves businesses across aerospace, defense, manufacturing, hardware, energy, and artificial intelligence. Erebor combines traditional deposit-taking and lending with a differentiated treasury management platform providing customers with a menu of yield across checking, savings, U.S. Treasuries, corporate bonds, stablecoins, and other instruments — all within a single regulated banking relationship. Founded in 2025 and headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, Erebor is the definitive financial partner for companies building the future. ‍

Everly Health offer services including over 30+ types of at-home lab testing kits and education, population-scale testing through a U.S.-wide clinician network, telehealth and a payer-supported/enterprise self-collected lab test. In May 2020, the company received the first FDA emergency use authorization of its kind for its COVID-19 Test Home Collection Kit.

Everspin Technologies, Inc. is the worldwide leader in designing, manufacturing, and commercially shipping discrete and embedded Magnetoresistive RAM (MRAM) and Spin-Torque MRAM (ST-MRAM) into markets and applications where data persistence and integrity, low latency, and security are paramount. With over 50 Million MRAM and ST-MRAM products deployed in data center, cloud storage, energy, industrial, automotive, and transportation markets, Everspin has built the strongest and fastest growing foundation of MRAM users in the world.

ESM3 is the first generative AI model for biology that simultaneously reasons over the sequence, structure, and function of proteins. This enables scientists to understand and create new proteins, making biology programmable.

Evolv is developing powerful, low-cost, physical threat-detection technology to keep people and places safe across the globe. A key component of Evolv's solution is Mosaiq, a next-generation artificial intelligence platform. Mosaiq produces insight from real-world, real-time video streams by combining state-of-the-art AI with a global network of human trainers.
Fiddler enables developers and users to continuously understand, analyze, validate and monitor their AI systems at scale, reducing the traditionally "black box" nature of machine learning models.

Founded by former Rambus CEO Geoff Tate, Flex Logix is creating cores for field programmable gate arrays (FPGAs) that allow a chip to change its identity even after it has been shipped. Flex Logix will be the first to provide chipmakers with the power of programmable logic without the cost, performance, and power penalties typically associated with FPGAs. The technology will enable cheap, portable devices to reconfigure themselves to offer a range of functionalities, from rich graphics and rendering to powerful computer vision.

Founded in 2016, Flutterwave has built the premier cross-border fintech API for the African continent and beyond, starting with Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, South Africa and many other countries. Its platform can accept payments both online and at point-of-sale devices using a variety of methods such as cash, credit, mobile money, M-Pesa, USSD, and Visa QR. In addition, Flutterwave offers card issuing, invoicing, store building, and other critical commerce services built on top of its payments engine, making it a one-stop shop for businesses looking for a better way to send and accept money.
With Formic, anyone can start using automation - no in-house engineering expertise, high upfront investment of time or capital required.

With certified expert guidance, users can track their progress and see their entire financial picture in real time, anytime. Fruitful doesn't sell products on commission or skim “management” fees while doing nothing. Instead, it gives you clarity and control over your money for a flat, affordable subscription. With Fruitful Guides, each user can get access to a Certified Financial Planner on-demand through the company's app.

G2X Energy is dedicated to converting abundant, low-cost natural gas resources into liquid fuels. With a huge potential market, the ability to solve many of the U.S.’s energy challenges, and a leadership team that is experienced in acting on opportunities, we are well positioned for growth and success.
Galileo provides anytime, anywhere access to expert doctors—no matter the health need, medical question, or chronic concern.

Gameto's scientific work involves reconstituting ovarian development and function in vitro, elucidating the underlying growth factor and genetic networks that drive human ovarian development and oogenesis. We aim to leverage our allogeneic human cellular derivatives of our platform to therapeutic applications for assisted fertility and menopause.
Gandeeva is harnessing cryo-electron microscopy and machine learning to advance drug discovery.

Genocea is harnessing the power of T cell immunity to develop life-changing vaccines and immunotherapies. T cells are increasingly recognized as a critical element of protective immune responses to a wide range of diseases. Traditional discovery methods have proven unable to identify the targets of immune response. ATLAS, Genocea's proprietary technology platform, mimics the human immune system to identify the targets most likely to illicit an immune response. Genocea's pipeline of novel, clinical-stage, T cell-enabled product candidates includes GEN-003 for genital herpes, GEN-004 for pneumococcus, and earlier-stage programs in chlamydia, malaria and cancer immunotherapy.

Goldsky's platform empowers developers to pull data from a project or directly from a chain, filter and chain the results into their ideal shape, and query their API, use webhooks, or export to their backend. Its serverless architecture ensures that developers can focus on the logic of their software, and not on figuring out how to scale up their infrastructure.

H1 maintains a comprehensive database of medical professionals and service providers, including their expertise, interests, publications, location, speaking engagements and involvement in clinical trials.
Hadrian manufactures critical precision components in autonomous factories for space and defense, halving the cost of building rockets, satellites, jets and drones.

Halo Neuroscience uses proven, proprietary, non-invasive neurostimulation technology targeting the brain to enhance performance for the able and impaired. The company's first product, Halo Sport, is a headset that uses pulses of energy to improve the brain’s response to training by causing stronger, more direct connections to be formed between neurons and muscles.

Happiest Baby is a mission-driven company dedicated to developing science-based products and services to improve the health, safety and emotional well-being of children and parents around the world. Founded by renowned pediatrician and best-selling author Dr. Harvey Karp, Happiest Baby, Inc. is developing a suite of smart solutions for new parents based on Dr. Karp’s landmark insights into the neurodevelopment of young children.
Higharc allows home owners and developers to custom design their homes in 3D to the lot they want to build on, and get accurate cost estimates and build-ready plans on the spot.
HiHello's first product is a free digital business card and contact manager application.

Hugging Face created Transformers, the most popular open-source platform for developers and scientists to build state-of-the-art natural language processing technologies including text classification, information extraction, summarization, text generation and conversational artificial intelligence.
HumanFirst is building a platform to review and dispense connected technologies to support safe, effective, and personalized adoption of biometric monitoring in clinical trials and remote care.

Launched in September 2021 by Founder and CEO Tom Mueller, a founding member of SpaceX, Impulse Space is driven by a vision to provide economical last-mile space payload delivery services spanning in-space transportation, orbital transport of satellites to optimal orbits, in-orbit servicing, space debris deorbit and space station orbit keeping.

Inductive Bio is a technology company developing a machine learning (ML) platform designed to dramatically accelerate the compound optimization process, a critical and time-consuming step in developing new therapeutics. By building datasets and state-of-the-art ML models designed to map the drivers of small molecule Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion, and Toxicity (ADMET), Inductive's platform allows scientists to optimize initial compounds into leads and development candidates faster and with a better balance of ADMET properties.

Instock is delivering the first fully as-a-Service goods-to-person (G2P) system for eCommerce operations. Instock's breakthrough technology addresses market demands with their software-defined automation, simulation-first approach, and embedded self-service for day to day operations. With Instock, G2P becomes accessible to every fulfillment business via radically improved flexibility, modularity and price point.

Ironclad automates the administrative work around creating and negotiating contracts to establish a single-source of truth, enabling their customers to search, analyze, and better understand all their contracts.

Kala was co-founded by Lux in 2009 alongside recognized experts in the field of nanomedicine, including professors Justin Hanes and Robert Langer, and pharmaceutical industry veteran Dr. Colin Gardner. The company is a clinical-stage pharmaceutical company focused on innovative, nanoparticle-based treatments for ocular diseases that affect the front and back of the eye. Kala leverages its proprietary mucus-penetrating, particle technology to develop topical ophthalmic formulations with enhanced delivery into ocular tissue. These treatments have the potential to provide greater efficacy over longer periods of time and replace injections with a simple eye drop.

Kallyope is a biotechnology company co-founded by Lux with serial entrepreneurs Charles Zuker (co-founder of Aurora Biosciences and Senomyx), Tom Maniatis (Lasker Award winner and co-founder of Genetics Institute, New York Genome Center, ProScript Inc, and Acceleron Pharma) and Richard Axel (2004 Nobel Prize winner). Kallyope integrates cutting-edge technologies in sequencing, bioinformatics, neural imaging, cellular and molecular biology and human genetics to provide an understanding of gut-brain biology that will lead to transformational therapeutics and products that improve human health and nutrition.

Kela is a secure, open platform enabling rapid and seamless integration of commercial and military systems in service of Western defense. Designed and battle-tested by Israeli innovators, Kela ensures the West meets today’s frontline challenges and evolves with tomorrow’s innovations.

Born with a contrarian view, Lux founded and funded Kurion alongside a team of extraordinary industry talent to solve the single biggest known—but neglected problem—in energy and environment. Just one year after Lux led the company's seed financing, Kurion won a transformative contract from TEPCO to use its advanced technology for the Fukushima nuclear plant cleanup. The Kurion system has been successfully working to remove more than 99% of the radioactivity from contaminated water, helping to avert a nuclear meltdown.

Metamaterials are a new class of man-made materials that are engineered to exhibit optical, electrical and other properties that cannot be found in nature. Kymeta is commercializing a new form of metamaterials-based satellite antenna. Kymeta’s mTenna products simplify the connection between mobile users and high-capacity communications satellites. The technology has been heralded as the biggest breakthrough in satellite communications in the last 50 years.

Kyruus delivers proven provider search and scheduling solutions that help hospitals and health systems match patients with the providers best suited to care for them. The ProviderMatch suite of solutions—for consumers, access centers, and referral networks—enables a consistent patient experience across multiple points of access, while aligning provider supply with patient demand. The company’s proprietary provider data management platform forms the foundation of its solutions, powering them with accurate data by coupling data processing with administrative applications.

LabGenius develops therapeutics of high value for diseases of unmet need. The company deploys machine intelligence to model the relationship between DNA sequence and protein function, while improving the predictive accuracy of these models through experimental lab data generated through its smart robotic platform.

LangChain is the platform for developers to build context aware AI reasoning apps from prototype to production. LangChain provides developers a framework to construct LLM powered apps, give visibility into what's happening and serve applications.

Thousands of years ago, locks were invented to keep people out. And they’ve done a pretty good job so far. But as our lives grow more complicated—busier, faster, fuller—there are more people coming in and out of our doors. That’s where locks start to seem restrictive. The challenge is no longer keeping people out: it’s letting the right people in when we can’t be there to do it ourselves.Technology has enabled the rise of an on-demand economy that relies upon seamless exchange of information, goods and services. On top of that is the need for trust. Today, that trust stops at the front door. Latch is building the first universal platform to enable trusted parties to access physical spaces, simply and securely, starting with the apartment and office buildings where we live and work. Latch understands the need for security and reliability, but also the importance of convenience, simplicity, and ease of use. By embedding intelligence at the point of access, Latch is building for a world where any space — be it a 1,000-unit property, personal home, or post box — can be securely and seamlessly accessed.

The CRISPR community is facing a tsunami of data but a dearth of computational tools required for their analyses. By empowering the researcher, Latch is helping remove a significant bottleneck that is currently slowing down the CRISPR research cycle, enabling the discovery of new therapies.

From their offices in Greenpoint, Brooklyn and Hong Kong, Looking Glass is bridging the gap between virtual space and the real world with their line of Looking Glass holographic displays powered by proprietary light field technology. The science-fiction dream of interactive and group-viewable holograms becomes real with Looking Glass, as it enables the creation, interaction, and distribution of three-dimensional holographic content without AR/VR headgear. In late 2019 the company broke new ground with the launch of the world’s largest and highest-resolution holographic display: the Looking Glass 8K Immersive Display.

Lumafield takes the friction out of inspection with Neptune, a groundbreaking easy-to-use CT scanner paired with Voyager, powerful cloud-based software that solves problems from initial design through mass manufacturing.

Lumaril is focused on building and deploying an AI platform for industrial automation -- codifying operator expertise and running on event-driven data to make near-real-time operational decisions across production facilities. We're building a platform to bring generative AI to large industrial systems. Our focus is on running deep learning models on complex event-based data — enabling more adaptive, intelligent control across the physical operations of companies.

Founded by Lux Capital in 2004, Lux Research helps leaders in business, finance, and government make informed, strategic decisions. Through its proprietary methodologies and global network of innovators, Lux Research uncovers opportunities and provides its readers with competitive advantages. Lux Research was acquired by Bregal Sagemount in 2017.

Luxtera brings direct “fiber to the chip” connectivity to market. It is the first company to overcome the complex technical obstacles involved with integrating high-performance optics directly with silicon electronics on a mainstream CMOS chip. Luxtera's proprietary design and fabrication processes combine standard transistors for digital and analog electronic circuitry with passive nano-photonic optical structures. These CMOS Photonic transceivers offer better performance, increased reliability, and reduced power consumption at a fraction of the cost of traditional optical assemblies.

The average healthcare provider works with 25 payers. Providers must be enrolled with payers to receive payments. Each payer requires a unique set of forms, procedures, and data to be submitted. It takes months for payers to fully process provider enrollments – and months before providers start receiving correct payments. Time is wasted dealing with paper forms, correcting manual errors, and tracking down the status of enrollments. Madaket automates the enrollment process. Providers fill out a simple online form once, and Madaket sends the right information to each applicable payer. The result? Less paperwork and faster payment.
Magen Biosciences developed effective therapies for a number of skin disorders and the prevention of UV radiation related skin damage.

Mahana Therapeutics is developing a brand new class of digital therapeutics treatments for patients suffering from gastrointestinal diseases such as Irritable Bowel Syndrome, Crohn’s Disease and Ulcerative Colitis.

Majestic Labs builds power-efficient AI servers for the largest and most advanced AI workloads. The company's flagship server architecture collapses multiple racks of conventional equipment into a single server. Majestic’s system features custom accelerator and memory interface chips that disaggregate memory from compute, enabling up to 128 TB of extremely fast, power-efficient, high-bandwidth memory per server, nearly 100 times more than today's leading GPU servers. This breakthrough allows organizations to run massive AI models while dramatically reducing power consumption, data center footprint, and infrastructure costs. Majestic Labs' mission is to democratize access to the most advanced AI capabilities and reduce the environmental impact of AI infrastructure.

Matterport is an immersive-media technology company that builds 3D media solutions. The company serves multiple industries, including real estate, construction, retail and entertainment. Its team has built the first end-to-end media platform that allows users to easily create, modify, navigate, and build on digital representations of real places. Founded in 2011, Matterport's vision is to create a complete virtual model of the world that changes the way individuals and industries interact with physical spaces.

Maven is the largest virtual clinic for women’s and family health, offering continuous, holistic care for fertility, pregnancy, and parenting. Maven’s award-winning digital programs are trusted by leading employers and health plans to reduce costs and drive better health outcomes for both parents and children.

Maven AGI is a platform building AI agents to transform enterprise customer experience through the power of Generative AI. Maven AGI seamlessly integrates with existing systems, handling multiple modalities in over 50 languages with ease while drawing from a comprehensive understanding of past interactions, transactions, and user needs. They're reimagining customer service from the ground up, delivering personalized user experiences with unprecedented accuracy and efficiency.

Medra combines the flexibility of general-purpose robots with the reasoning capabilities of large language models to transform science. Our Physical AI platform iterates on real experiments and improves scientific outcomes for our customers. We partner with leading biotech and pharma companies to design new antibodies, develop gene therapies, run cell-based assays, and more. ‍
Mendaera was founded to address inefficiencies and unmet needs in healthcare using advanced technology.

Miga Health is building a consumer brand to provide the best preventative care to all Americans. Heart and cardiovascular disease is one of the most common problems afflicting Americans particularly as they age, but the right, consistent care can stave off strokes and heart attacks as well as extend quality of life.
Modal helps people run code in the cloud and is the easiest way for developers to get access to containerized, serverless compute.

Molecular Imprints was formed to commercialize a new type of imprint lithography. This unique nanoscale patterning capability enables new devices within the semiconductor, flat-panel display, hard-disk-drive, life science, and other high technology industries. Molecular Imprints sold its semiconductor imprint lithography equipment business to Canon Inc. in 2014, and its remaining business was acquired by a private company in 2015.

Moment believes that the future of photography is in our pockets. The company makes an ecosystem of products that work together to make the mobile phone a better camera. The company's first products include the world's highest-quality mobile lenses, cases that add dedicated photo controls, and an app that communicates with cases and lenses to enhance the mobile picture-taking experience.The company was formed in 2013 by Marc Barros, a serial entrepreneur who previously founded and ran hands-free camera company Contour, Inc.
Mos' breakthrough technology helps more students get more of the government's $135B in annual college financial aid with a single application.

The MosaicML Composer is an open source deep learning library purpose-built to make it easy to add algorithmic methods and compose them together into novel recipes that speed up model training and improve model quality.

MPharma disrupts the African prescription drug supply industry by stocking pharmacy shelves with no payment required up front. It uses the collective power of its network of pharmacies to negotiate lower prices with the best manufacturers, and it harnesses technology to eliminate the inefficiencies and price fluctuations that prevent prescription drugs reaching sick people.

Multiply Labs’ robotic systems leverage the latest advancements in autonomous technology – from collaborative robots to cloud-controlled fleets – to enable the production of individualized drugs at industrial scale.

Using digital/analog hybridization to compute inside memory, Mythic's unique local Artificial Intelligence platform puts desktop GPU compute capabilities and deep neural networks onto a button-sized chip. This entirely new approach performs the inference step of deep neural networks inside the memory array that stores the processing weight long term – bringing huge advantages in performance, power life and accuracy.

Nanosys is leading the development of quantum dot technology for displays. The company has partnered with top display manufacturers to produce standout tablets, TVs and smartphones that are thinner, lighter, brighter and more colorful than ever before. Nanosys technology is covered by a portfolio of over 500 patents and applications that is currently being applied to multiple industries including flat-panel displays and batteries.

Neo is taking on the byzantine problem of small business accounting, applying novel techniques in NLP to automate analog processes and democratize access to capital for scientific and technological innovations.

Artificial intelligence (AI) techniques are rivaling human performance and will be the foundation of the next revolution in computing. Nervana Systems is building the first optimized, full-stack platform for machine intelligence. Built for ease of use, speed, and scale, Nervana’s brain-inspired deep learning solution abstracts away the complexities associated with AI development. This allows users to focus on building solutions that tackle the world’s toughest machine-learning problems.

Using DNA nanotechnology, spectral multiplexing, and automation, Nomic's nELISA platform transforms the traditional sandwich immunoassay into a massively multiplexed protein profiling tool without compromising throughput, cost, or fidelity.

Nominal accelerates hardware test campaigns with tools designed for rapid validation, observability, and advanced data analysis. We empower commercial companies and DoD institutions to quickly reach their milestones, while building resilience and reliability at every step of the way.

Traditional cybersecurity solutions are not effective with ICS (industrial controls systems), and as more come online, they have become vulnerable to threats that are hard to detect, design against and prevent. Nozomi Networks has been delivering innovative cybersecurity and operational visibility solutions for ICS since 2013. By applying network behavioral analytics to ICS environments, Nozomi Networks’ flagship product, SCADAguardian delivers real-time visibility into process network communications and configurations. Its ICS network mapping and automated process analysis detects cyber-attacks and operational missteps for immediate remediation.
Oasis allows customers to store and analyze sensitive user data, while allowing customers to retain ownership and privacy.

Octant is developing a multiplexed technology that uses synthetic biology, genome engineering, next-generation sequencing, and computational tools to simultaneously measure the activity of thousands of receptor pathways in human cells, paving the way for a new modality for drug discovery.
OpenSpace combines cameras, computer vision and graphics to allow people on job sites to efficiently capture their work and analyze it, reducing the cost and time it takes to build things.

Longer-term, OpenStore aims to bring the experience of spontaneous discovery back online – ultimately connecting merchants and customers into a single unified shopping experience through access to data, information, and capital.

Orbital seeks to understand global and national trends through advanced image-processing and data science at petabyte scale. By analyzing millions of satellite images at a time, Orbital Insight equips industry leaders with advanced, unbiased knowledge of socio-economic trends.

Vision and hearing have been digitized — but not smell — our oldest and deepest sense. In digitizing scent, Osmo’s team has built history’s first map of odor – a way to predict what a molecule smells like from its structure. Osmo is now using this map and Generative AI to create a new generation of better, safer, environmentally-friendly aroma molecules for the ~$30 billion flavor and fragrance market.
Paceline incentivizes active behavior through a platform that integrates wearable devices, preventative health practices and partnerships with leading wellness brands.

Pager gives people access to high-quality, personal healthcare, and empowers patients to take better care of themselves. Bringing doctor house calls into the 21st century, patients can view and select Pager-partnered, hospital-associated doctors and schedule telemedicine or in person appointments using a mobile application. The company leverages excess physician-capacity by partnering with large hospital systems, urgent care facilities and pharmacy retail clinics. Additional services, including preventative flu vaccines, back-to-school physicals, and physical therapy rehabilitation, fulfill the last mile of health and wellness-care delivery in the country.

Paradigm is building the platform to empower the future of clinical trials. We’re transforming a fragmented and inefficient approach to clinical research into a high-performance trial ecosystem. Our platform will accelerate recruitment, speed study-related insights, automate operations, and make it easy for any physician anywhere to evaluate patients for clinical trials.

Pawp is a first-of-its-kind platform that addresses two essential pain points for pet parents: access to quality vet care and help paying for it. For a low monthly membership fee, Pawp offers on-demand telehealth via a 24/7 digital vet clinic, and an emergency fund so pet owners are never forced to make difficult healthcare decisions based on accessibility or financial restraints.

Perchwell is the next generation data and workflow platform for the residential real estate industry. Perchwell structures a wide array of property related data and offers real estate professionals a comprehensive workflow toolset on top of that data designed to empower them to thrive in today’s evolving marketplace.
Performance Drone Works leverages proprietary advances in drone technology to help inform and protect our men and women in uniform.

Phonic is the next-generation voice AI platform to build reliable voice agents. In the future, we believe that voice agents will feel as natural and as reliable as humans. We're a team of researchers, engineers, and product dreamers based out of San Francisco.
Physera’s licensed physical therapists leverage technology to treat patients remotely, improve outcomes, and reduce healthcare costs.

Physical Intelligence is bringing general-purpose AI into the physical world. We are a group of engineers, scientists, roboticists, and company builders developing foundation models and learning algorithms to power the robots of today and the physically-actuated devices of the future.

Healthcare needs good data. At PicnicHealth, we are building deep real-world datasets fueling cutting edge research while giving patients control of their own medical record data. These complete, clinically-rich datasets produce unique insights - across dozens of diseases - to ultimately get the right treatments into patients’ hands faster. We do this by working directly with patients and leveraging state of the art machine learning and human curation to transform messy medical records into structured, research-ready datasets. To date, we’ve helped tens of thousands of patients securely access their records and proactively contribute to advancing research in diseases that impacts their lives.

Pinscreen is a computational imaging startup founded by USC Professor and former Industrial Light & Magic scientist Hao Li. The company is revolutionizing digital communication and online human interactions with user-generated and personalized 3D virtual avatars.

Pivotal Commware recognized that wireless demand for data would outgrow the complex coding, multiplexing and modulation schemes that network operators have deployed thus far. Beamforming was the answer, but existing high cost, size, weight and power-consuming solutions were not. That’s why Pivotal assembled a team experienced in advanced commercial antenna and RF circuit design, advanced DARPA technology programs, and, uniquely, active nonlinear metamaterials.

Founded in 2010 by a team of ex-NASA scientists, Planet Labs is driven by a mission to image the entire Earth every day, and provide universal access to that data. Planet Labs satellites are collecting a radical new data set with endless, real-world applications such as measuring agricultural yields, monitoring natural resources and aiding first responders after natural disasters. By operating the largest-ever constellation of Earth-imaging satellites, Planet Labs believes that timely, global imagery will empower informed, deliberate and meaningful stewardship of our planet.

The company's DELPhe platform, named for DNA-Encoded Library (DEL) phenotypic (Phe) screening, is a unique drug discovery engine designed to identify novel small molecules for multiple indications, including cancer and inflammatory disease indications.

Primer is a machine intelligence company that uses machine learning and natural language processing to automate the analysis of large datasets. Primer builds systems that read documents, discover insights and automatically generate reports comparable to those of a human analyst.

Ramp's software platform consolidates companies' financial software stacks, offering tools that automate expense management, bill payment, accounting, controls and approval, real-time reporting as well as integrations with existing systems.

Recursion Pharma is a Salt Lake City-based drug discovery company founded in 2013 based on technology developed at the University of Utah. Recursion uses a novel drug screening platform to efficiently reposition known drugs and shelved pharmaceutical assets to treat rare genetic diseases and other conditions.

Reflect Orbital is building a satellite constellation to deliver sunlight on demand. Using reflectors and large deployable structures, we transform small satellites into precise orbital mirrors. We are creating a new category of space-enabled infrastructure, our technology serves energy resilience, nighttime lighting, defense, and beyond. This enables instant, infrastructure-free lighting and energy augmentation anywhere on Earth, without the need for fuel, wires, or new installations.Our technology unlocks new possibilities across multiple sectors. In the energy sector, we extend solar production beyond daylight hours, increasing output and reliability for utilities, data centers, power producers, and 24/7 clean power buyers. For our industrial customers, we provide powerful nighttime lighting for remote construction, mining, and logistics operations. We support emergency response and defense missions with real-time, controllable illumination in austere environments, at a moment's notice. In agriculture we enable longer growing seasons; for civil lighting we offer improved safety, extended daytime hours, and equitable access to light. As our constellation scales, Reflect Orbital will create space-enabled infrastructure, bringing light wherever and whenever it’s needed, with global reach.

Reflex is a platform to build web apps in pure Python, empowering developers to build their whole web app seamlessly (both frontend and backend) providing the flexibility of a traditional web framework with the simplicity of one language and one framework for the full stack. Reflex allows Python developers to become full-stack engineers without learning a new toolchain like JavaScript plus React and Next.js.

Relativity is creating the first autonomous rocket factory + launch service. We are an orbital launch company that will deploy and resupply satellite constellations with industry-defining lead time and cost. Our entirely new process to build and fly rockets will redefine how we access space to connect our planet. Relativity's technology builds toward our long-term goal of 3D printing the first rocket made on Mars.

In light of the pandemic, manufacturing supply chains are needed that can withstand disruptive shocks and safeguard health security. Resilience is changing the way medicine is made – leveraging innovation to better serve scientific discovery, withstand disruptive events, and reach those in need.

Rigetti Computing is building a cloud quantum computing platform for artificial intelligence and computational chemistry. Rigetti's quantum API, dubbed "Forest", emphasizes a quantum-classical hybrid computing model, integrating directly with existing cloud infrastructure and treating the quantum computer as an accelerator.

Ripcord is the world’s first robotic digitization company. The company uses sophisticated robotic automation and software to provide customers with a secure, fast, all-inclusive records management solution.

Healthcare providers are the critically important middlemen of a broken healthcare system. The revenue cycle management suites they rely on to help them get paid accurately and efficiently were invented for the era they were built in: decades ago. Technology has advanced, communication methods have changed, payers are pinching reimbursement rates and patients are responsible for a large percentage of their care costs. Rivet provides an elegant, modern software platform to centralize and automate healthcare transactions.
Pioneering new techniques for image and video creation, Runway is inventing the future of creative tools with machine intelligence - reimagining how we create, so we can create impossible things.

Scientific research vessels with human crews cost about $80,000 per day. Saildrone's fleet of autonomous sailboats collect vast amounts of ocean data year-round for a fraction of the cost of traditional research ships. Saildrones have now sailed over 60,000 nautical miles, with missions in the Atlantic, Pacific, Gulf of Mexico and Bering Sea.
Sakana AI is a Tokyo-based R&D company on a quest to build a new kind of foundation model based on nature-inspired intelligence.

Science 37 is transforming the clinical research process by shifting the center for research from traditional academic sites to the patient’s home and local healthcare system. The platform is the industry’s first and only comprehensive, integrated platform purpose-built to support the systems, workflows, and processes of virtual trials.
Senti Biosciences leverages expertise in cell engineering and biological programming to build the future of gene and cell based therapies.

Shapeways sits at the forefront of 3D printing technology. The company provides access for individuals and small businesses to real production capability so they can create and sell their own products without needing to build their own manufacturing facilities. The company operates the world's largest marketplace and fulfillment engine for 3D-printed goods. With millions of unique products for sale in its marketplace, Shapeways leverages sophisticated software and state-of-the-art 3D printing facilities to efficiently produce objects on demand. As 3D printing technology continues to become less expensive, more capable, and more accessible, Shapeways and its community will benefit from the ability to offer more products in more materials at better prices. The Shapeways team is pioneering a transformative opportunity to make products (and production) personal.
The company uses computational tools to discover non-animal proteins whose structure makes them well-suited to a potential new food.

SiBEAM is a fabless semiconductor company that provides integrated circuits and system solutions for millimeter-wave wireless communications and sensing. SiBEAM was founded to commercialize pioneering millimeter wave wireless technology developed at the labs of University of California, Berkeley. SiBEAM was acquired by Silicon Image in 2011.

Silicon Clocks was a fabless semiconductor electronics company developing timing chips for hard disk drives, disk controllers, communications devices, and other consumer electronics. The technology eliminated the need for off-chip quartz crystal resonators, which resulted cost reduction and a 10x decrease in power dissipation for the timing component. The company was acquired by Silicon Labs in 2011.

Siluria Technologies is pioneering the commercial production of fuels and chemicals made from clean, abundant, natural gas. Siluria’s breakthrough Oxidative Coupling of Methane ("OCM") process technology is believed to be the first commercially-viable process to directly convert methane to ethylene. Siluria’s second process technology can convert ethylene to liquid fuels such as gasoline, diesel or jet fuel. This enables natural gas to potentially supplement petroleum as the worldwide basis for transportation fuels and commodity chemicals. Siluria’s revolutionary catalyst and process technologies uniquely combine nanomaterials, templating and chemical engineering to convert natural gas into higher-value products using efficient processes that can be seamlessly integrated into existing industry infrastructure.

SOLS leverages advanced 3D scanning, automated product generation, and digital manufacturing as the foundation of a scalable platform for custom mass manufacturing. For its first product, SOLS’s proprietary computer vision system translates still images into a detailed, highly precise 3D model of the foot. When combined with information about a patient’s body, lifestyle, and medical needs, the data is used to generate a completely custom, precision-engineered wearable device that improves comfort and performance. Sols was acquired by Aetrex in 2017.
Sourcegraph enables developers and managers to search and explore all of their organization’s code on the web, with integrations into their existing tools.

The Stacks Foundation manages the open-source infrastructure for the Stacks ledger, with associated project Hiro building developer tools for that network to ultimately enable apps and smart contracts on Bitcoin.

More people play competitive video games than American football, yet no true data-driven methods exist to track player performance. Statespace's first product, Aim Lab, brings neuroscience research into a training simulator built from the ground up to quantify player skill and help them get better, faster. The unique data the company collects allows it to create powerful machine learning algorithms to classify player skillsets, personality traits, and play-style biases. These insights lead to entirely new opportunities in player scouting, matchmaking, fantasy betting, and cheat detection.
For consumers, SteadyMD is your personal doctor, online. For businesses, the company's technology backbone powers white-label telehealth services for enterprises across all 50 states.

Stord is the cloud supply chain enabling companies to compete and grow with world-class logistics – including warehousing, freight and fulfillment – in a single, integrated platform that’s available exactly when and where they need it.

Through combining best-in-class robotics and control systems and advanced software for analytics, the Strateos Robotic Cloud Lab platform enables unparalleled scale, efficiency, and accuracy for the advancement of drug discovery and synthetic biology.

Summer Health provides parents access to reliable, fast and asynchronous pediatric care for their children. Simply text a pediatric question or concern and a doctor will respond within 15 minutes to provide support. It's that simple.

Spun out of the University of Southern California (USC), Survios was founded by a group of developers, creative artists, and gamers with a vision to make VR consoles as ubiquitous as televisions worldwide.

Tactic's platform provides a clear view of an organization’s treasury and a robust transaction audit trail in an accounting-friendly format. Tactic works seamlessly with existing custody solutions such as Coinbase and traditional financial software like QuickBooks.
Tecton is the first enterprise-ready data platform to help teams operationalize machine learning and enable data science and engineering to collaborate efficiently.

Today’s electronics factories push low-volume orders through a high-volume process, resulting in a slow, tedious and expensive customer experience. Tempo Automation introduces software that coaches electrical engineers on design for manufacturing and backs it up with an advanced robotic factory. As a result, creators of autonomous cars, satellites, connected devices and other cutting edge products can frictionlessly get to market.
Tendo is a healthcare cloud software company providing a digital engagement platform and suite of applications that connect patients, clinicians, and caregivers throughout the care cycle.

Tenzai introduces an AI agentic penetration testing platform that actively hacks, exploits, and fixes hardest-to-find vulnerabilities across enterprise software at the speed of AI. It actively hacks, exploits, and helps fix vulnerabilities across enterprise software - continuously and at scale.

Thatch is on a mission to help startups give great healthcare to their teams. The platform uses financial infrastructure to create a unified interface for healthcare spending and saving, helping startups provide competitive health and wellness benefits for their teams in less time.

At Thematic, we're experts at providing the latest institutional AI-powered research and product development capabilities to the asset and investment management industry. We take advantage of the up-to-date capabilities of ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama; with industry-context prompt engineering; and financial and market data to enable investment professionals to efficiently and confidently develop a point of view on a company or investment theme.

Thrive combines cutting-edge liquid biopsy technology with a machine learning engine to find multiple cancer types from a single blood draw — including many cancers that lack effective screening tools such as ovarian, pancreatic, liver cancers and others.
Timber allows customers to collect, transform, route and otherwise take control of their observability data with one simple tool.
Tines is a no-code security automation platform designed to allow anyone to automate any repetitive manual task, regardless of complexity.

Together’s mission is to empower innovation and creativity by providing leading open-source generative AI models and a cloud platform that makes AI accessible to anyone, anywhere. They aim to help create open models that out rival closed models and establish open-source as the default way to incorporate AI. Leveraging research in distributed optimization, Together built a specialized cloud platform for large models that efficiently scales training and inference.

Transphorm is a global semiconductor company that develops gallium nitride (GaN) transistors and modules for high-voltage power conversion applications. Built on an industry-leading IP portfolio and over 300 years of combined GaN engineering expertise, Transphorm is delivering the highest performance and highest reliability GaN devices and best-in-class applications-driven design support to a growing customer base. Transphorm is creating innovations that move beyond the limitations of silicon to capture 90% of today’s energy losses.

Community oncologists – who typically work at small- to medium-sized physician-owned practices – treat almost half of all patients with cancer. Trial Library is introducing a new approach to the community-engaged research model by creating a set of tools for providers to get paid for their work in screening for trials, becoming true partners with investigators at trial sites. This solves the “first mile problem” of clinical trial engagement, and it's coupled with the company's patient navigation support tools to solve the “last mile problem” in order to provide the complete solution this industry has lacked.

Unconventional AI is rethinking the foundations of a computer to optimize energy efficiency for AI. Founded by experts in AI systems, analog circuits, computing theory, and neuroscience, we are bringing biology-scale efficiency to artificial intelligence.‍

Until is building reversible cryopreservation technology. We develop perfusion systems, cryoprotectant formulations, controlled-rate freezers, and vitrification and rewarming protocols for biological systems. Our focus today is organ-scale reversible cryopreservation: preserving donated organs at subzero temperatures without ice formation, then rewarming them uniformly for transplant. By solving this time-critical challenge, we’re laying the foundation for whole-body reversible cryopreservation — pausing molecular motion to give patients a bridge to future cures.

Valthos builds next-generation biodefense. The company harnesses computational methods to characterize biological sequences and design adaptive countermeasures, helping government and life sciences stakeholders respond to emerging threats with speed and precision.
Varda is building the first space factory to launch and return space-made products for terrestrial applications.

Variant Bio is searching the globe for people who are medical or genomic outliers in order to identify the genes and pathways driving these traits, with the goal of uncovering the targets for tomorrow’s drugs and advancing much-needed therapies to market.

Using advanced computer vision and 3D sensing, Veo Robotics enables safe and dynamic interaction between humans and robots, allowing manufacturers to build more efficient and flexible workcells. Dynamic human-machine interaction will unlock new methods of production to meet modern demands of mass-customization, higher product variability and quality expectations, and faster product cycles.

Vesta Healthcare transforms the in-home care and aging experience by providing elderly clients with personalized care planning, expert caregiver matching, custom mobile technology, and proactive case management to improve older adults’ health and well-being while giving their families peace of mind.

Visor is a first-of-its-kind online tax filing and advisory service that removes the hassle and complexity of doing your taxes. In addition to preparing your annual return, Visor optimizes your finances by providing year-round tax advice at a flat fee.

Visterra uses its proprietary platform to identify unique disease targets and design and engineer effective therapeutics. The company’s technology is powered by computational tools and techniques that identify a specific area, or epitope, on the target site that is fundamental to its structure and function. This ideal epitope, or hierotope, becomes the target against which the company designs a novel therapeutic with the potential to effectively and durably combat the disease. The company is currently focused on therapeutics for infectious diseases. Its lead product candidate is a broad-spectrum, human, monoclonal antibody for the treatment of both seasonal and pandemic influenza. The company’s second product candidate is a human monoclonal antibody for the treatment of dengue that has been shown to broadly neutralize all four Dengue virus serotypes. Visterra was founded based on scientific work developed in the laboratory of Dr. Ram Sasisekharan, and licensed from MIT.

Vium empowers biomedical investigators with technology that accelerates the preclinical drug discovery and development pipeline. Researchers can design, run, and analyze experiments that rapidly surface insights and high volumes of quality data. The result is that better therapies get moved more quickly through to patients who need them.

Founded in 2019, Vosbor's flagship trading platform connects trading firms and end users across markets in a secure and transparent environment, allowing them to communicate and trade corn, wheat, soybean, palm oil and 14 other agricultural commodities. Vosbor is based in Amsterdam with additional offices in Singapore.

W4 Games wants to help the video game industry reclaim its control on the technology powering games, and reverse a dramatic trend where game developers have to rely on proprietary solutions from an ever-shrinking number of vendors. The company's aim is to make open-source software the industry’s first choice by ensuring that both enterprise and independent developers can use the community-developed Godot platform with peace of mind.

Waymark trains and deploys community based teams to work with patients and primary care providers. The company also partners with health plans to deliver a set of interventions that are highly effective, but frequently unavailable to Medicaid patients.

Whisper Aero is delivering the next generation of cleaner, quieter, more efficient thrust for drones, aircraft, and more. Today's open rotor propulsion systems are challenged by safety, noise, and performance limitations at all scales. Whisper's breakthrough technologies allow aircraft to fly 20x closer to the ground without disturbing communities, at speeds greater than 200 mph, and with incredible improvements in efficiency and cost.

Workit Health has built a best-in-class digital health platform, backed by clinical research, to help people overcome addictive behaviors and provide support to their loved ones. The Workit team is a group of mission-driven recovery and mental health enthusiasts who believe authenticity and innovation are the keys to revolutionizing help for addictive behaviors.
In the age of fake news, Yonder uses machine learning and AI to detect threats and provide brand manipulation protection before the damage is done.

As IoT data explodes, organizations need to process it at the edge before sending it to the cloud. Zededa offers a solution that delivers visibility, control and security for the enterprise and industrial edge.

Zipdrug is an on-demand prescription delivery service available online and through mobile applications. Patients enter payment and delivery details and snap a photo of their insurance card, and a HIPAA-trained Zipdrug messenger is dispatched to deliver the prescription(s). Zipdrug automatically applies any available discounts to the patient's medications reducing costs as a part of its service. The service is available for both one-time and recurring prescriptions. Patients can also choose to receive their medications from their preferred pharmacy.

Zoox is applying the latest in automotive, robotics and renewable energy technology to design a symmetrical, bidirectional, zero-emissions vehicle from the ground up to solve the unique challenges of autonomous mobility.
